Till (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $9,000,202Details
International Box Office $2,448,755
Worldwide Box Office $11,448,957
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $970,896
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $445,659
Total $1,416,555
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Mamie Till-Mobley is a widowed single mother who is the head of her household, the only Black woman working for the Air Force in Chicago. Till-Mobley becomes a revolutionary by insisting that the world witness the horror of her brutally maimed son’s body in an open casket viewing as an act of defiance against oppression and hate. “I wanted the world to see what they did to my boy," she said at the time. Till-Mobley also gave the exclusive rights to Jet Magazine to publish the images of her son’s maimed body which caused the lynching to gain worldwide noteriety. A mother’s audacity became a lightning rod in the Civil Rights Movement and propelled her to reluctantly become an outspoken activist for the NAACP advocating for social justice and education.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$242,269 (2.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.28 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:78.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:16 opening theaters/2,136 max. theaters, 3.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $10,222,451

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 14th, 2022 (Limited) by United Artists
October 28th, 2022 (Expands Wide) by United Artists
International Releases: January 6th, 2023 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 26th, 2023 (Wide) (Germany)
February 2nd, 2023 (Limited), released as Spravodlivosť pre Emmetta Tilla (Slovakia)
February 8th, 2023 (Wide) (France)
February 9th, 2023 (Wide), released as Till - A Busca por Justiça (Brazil)
Video Release: November 22nd, 2022 by United Artists Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic content involving racism, strong disturbing images and racial slurs.
(Rating bulletin 2748 (Cert #53730), 9/7/2022)
Running Time: 130 minutes
Geographical setting: Mississippi
Plot point: Murdered Child, Race Relations
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Time period setting: 1950s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Orion Pictures, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Mamie Till-Mobley is a widowed single mother who is the head of her household, the only Black woman working for the Air Force in Chicago. Till-Mobley becomes a revolutionary by insisting that the world witness the horror of her brutally maimed son’s body in an open casket viewing as an act of defiance against oppression and hate. “I wanted the world to see what they did to my boy," she said at the time. Till-Mobley also gave the exclusive rights to Jet Magazine to publish the images of her son’s maimed body which caused the lynching to gain worldwide noteriety. A mother’s audacity became a lightning rod in the Civil Rights Movement and propelled her to reluctantly become an outspoken activist for the NAACP advocating for social justice and education.
